title: Shortest Path in Weighted Graph
description:
Given a weighted directed graph as an adjacency list and two node IDs (start, end), find the shortest path distance. Return -1 if no path exists.

Graph format: {node: [[neighbor, weight], ...]}

Example:
  Input: graph={"A": [["B", 1], ["C", 4]], "B": [["C", 2]], "C": []}, start="A", end="C"
  Output: 3  (A→B→C = 1+2)

python.name: shortest_path
python.params: graph: dict, start: str, end: str

javascript.name: shortestPath
javascript.params: graph: object, start: string, end: string

hints: Dijkstra's algorithm with a priority queue
Use heapq in Python or a sorted array in JS
author: Claudius
